titleStmt.title: Letter from Foreman of Works to Charles Arthur Munro, 13 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: R. Gourlay

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from the Foreman of Works to Charles Arthur Munro (1869-1952), Governor of Mountjoy Prison. In this letter, the foreman reports that a large portion of a wall, which acts as the boundary between the prison and the property of the North Circular Laundry, fell during a storm on the previous night. The Foreman of works goes on to describe the dimensions of the wall and estimates that it is ten feet in height and two feet in width. The writer goes on to state that the wall has ‘the appearance of being badly built and very old'.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/2789

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Mountjoy Prison 13.11.15 I beg to report that during
the storm last night a portion
of the wall forming the boundary
between the grounds attached
to the Medical Officers quarters
and those of the North Circular
Road Laundry Company was
entirely blown down for a
length of 24 yards. no further
damage was caused. The wall
is 15 feet in height from the ground
and is built of rough rouble
stone and lime mortar, it is
2 feet thick and presents the
appearance of being badly
built and very old. R. Gourlay Foreman of Works I interviewed Mr. Abraham
re the matter. He tells
me the wall is clearly
the property of the Laundry Coy.
and that the city could
have been in a way responsible
for the injury to the wall
by the recent storm.
JN
2.12.15 The Governor
Submitted for the Board's
information. Estimate of repair of this wall
will be submitted as Vote responsible CAMunro
Governor
